questions.question: Just outside the surface of a solid bar carrying magnetizing current, the field strength:
options: Stays constant for several inches
Falls off rapidly, roughly in proportion to one over the distance from the bar
Increases with distance from the surface
Drops instantly to zero at the surface
questions.explanation: Inside the bar the field rises from zero at the centerline to a maximum at the surface; outside, it decays roughly as 1/distance — one reason leakage fields and lift-off matter so much.

questions.question: A 27 in. long, 3 in. diameter shaft gets a low fill-factor coil shot in a 5-turn coil. Using NI ≈ 45,000 ÷ (L/D), the expected coil current is about:
questions.explanation: L/D = 27 ÷ 3 = 9, so NI ≈ 45,000 ÷ 9 = 5,000 ampere-turns; with 5 turns the meter should read about 1,000 A — and a 27 in. part is magnetized in roughly 18 in. sections.

questions.question: Magnetizing units deliver thousands of amperes at only a few volts because:
options: High voltage would demagnetize the part
Magnetizing force depends on current, not voltage — voltage only has to push the current through the circuit resistance
Low voltage increases the skin effect
Ammeters cannot read high-voltage circuits
questions.explanation: H is set by amperes and geometry; a step-down transformer trades plant voltage for amperage, and voltage need only overcome cable, contact, and part resistance.

questions.question: Which statement about half-wave direct current (HWDC) is correct?
options: It penetrates less than AC because of the skin effect
Its unidirectional pulses penetrate nearly like DC while giving dry powder excellent mobility
It cannot be produced from line power
It is used only on wet horizontal bench units
questions.explanation: A single rectifier passes one half of the sine wave: the unidirectional average penetrates close to full DC, and the pulsation shakes dry powder into weak leakage fields — ideal for prod work on welds.

questions.question: A track shoe comes out of a furnace at about 800 °C. The crew wants an immediate MT check with high-temperature dry powder rated to 600 °F. What is wrong with the plan?
options: Nothing — the powder rating covers it
The steel is above its Curie point near 770 °C, so it is not ferromagnetic; no MT is possible until it cools below that, and the powder's 600 °F limit applies even later
Dry powder cannot be used on furnace parts
AC must be used on hot parts
questions.explanation: Above the Curie point the part cannot be magnetized at all; after it cools below roughly 770 °C the consumable's own temperature rating still controls when particles can be applied.

questions.question: Which single magnetic property must a part have for the residual method to produce reliable indications?
options: High electrical conductivity
High retentivity — the part must hold a strong residual field after the current stops
Low coercive force
Low permeability
questions.explanation: The residual method depends on the field left in the part after magnetization; only high-retentivity, wide-loop materials such as hardened high-carbon steels hold enough field to work.

questions.question: A drilled round hole and a tight crack of the same length sit side by side in a loaded plate. Which raises the local stress more, and why?
options: The hole — it removes more material
The crack — stress concentration grows as the discontinuity tip gets sharper, far exceeding the roughly 3× factor of a round hole
Both raise stress equally for equal length
Neither — local stress depends only on the average load
questions.explanation: Concentration depends on tip sharpness, not just size: a round hole runs about three times nominal stress, while a sharp crack tip concentrates stress far more severely.

questions.question: Smooth curved beach marks spreading from a point at the surface of a fracture face tell the failure analyst that:
options: The part failed in a single overload event
A fatigue crack grew progressively under cyclic loading from a surface origin before final fast fracture
The material was defect-free
The fracture started at the center of the section
questions.explanation: Beach marks are the signature of staged fatigue growth: initiation at a surface stress raiser, cycle-by-cycle propagation, then a rough final-fracture zone when the remaining section let go.

questions.question: Why is a central conductor used instead of a head shot to inspect the bore of hollow parts?
options: It uses less current for the same field
Current through the part's own wall leaves the bore surface with almost no field, while a conductor inside the bore makes the ID the strongest-field surface
Head shots only work with AC
A central conductor produces a longitudinal field for transverse flaws
questions.explanation: A hollow part carrying its own current encloses no current at the bore, so the ID sees nearly zero field; a central conductor moves the current inside the bore, magnetizing the ID strongly.

questions.question: A transverse fatigue crack is suspected mid-length on a 30 in. pump shaft. Which magnetization puts the flux in the right direction to find it?
options: Head shot — circular field around the shaft
Coil shot — longitudinal flux along the shaft axis crosses a transverse crack at 90°
Central conductor through a bore sleeve
Prods aligned along the shaft axis
questions.explanation: A transverse crack lies across the shaft, so it needs flux running along the axis — a coil or cable wrap. Circular techniques put flux in circles around the axis, parallel to the crack, and would miss it.
